trivia: The original 737 generation, with slim cigar-shaped JT8D engines tucked tight under the wing. Gravel-kit equipped -200s still serve remote northern strips no modern jet can touch.
If your station ever logs one, check the operator: it is almost certainly working somewhere wonderfully remote.
funFacts: Gravel kits let it operate from unpaved runways.
The engines are so slim because bypass ratios were tiny in 1967.
